title: Area-of-Origin Stringing
description: A pseudo-3D room of floor bloodstains. Add a string to each stain to project it back at its angle of impact, then find the 3D convergence zone and read the source's x, y, z position in metres.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Bloodstain Pattern Analysis
Area of Origin
Stringing Method
Angle of Impact
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — determine the 3D area of origin from stain direction and angle data.

educational.topic: Determining the 3D area of origin of a blood source by stringing
learningObjectives: Apply stain direction and angle-of-impact data to locate a source in 3D.
Explain why multiple stains are required to fix a single point of origin.
Interpret the convergence height as evidence about the source's position.
educational.prerequisites: Angle of impact (sin theta = W/L); basic 3D coordinates

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Build-up: click stains for data, add strings one at a time, find the intersection, and adjust source height to watch convergence move
pedagogical.assessment: Students locate the convergence zone and read its 3D coordinates
pedagogical.modelType: Axonometric room with strings constructed through a shared source point
controls: click a stain to select it
